textPlain: This title had the garden path sentence effect on me.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garden-path_sentence

Seems they've figured out how to wiggle themselves forward using their tails, then land in a way that creates a suction between their belly and the rocks. Clever.https://www.researchgate.net/publication/394405635_Bumblebee...
Caveman brain:  "That'd be a great spot to sit and just pick up a few fish for lunch."

The original title is plural: "Thousands of climbing catfish filmed scaling waterfalls"The word "catfish" can be singular or plural. I read it as "A catfish was filmed...". But there sure are a lot of those little fish.

> ...researchers also found that the [wet-rock-climbing] bumblebee catfish isn’t alone. Three other fish species were also scaling the waterfalls alongside the bumblebee catfish, and none of the four had ever been documented climbing before.Being fish, this sounds like convergent evolution.  (Vs. learned behaviors.)
Huh, interesting. Doesn't happen much in fusional languages.
